title: trigger generated for `always @*` in `write_verilog` is broken if a wire named `initial` already exists

features.name: always_ff with reserved keyword wire in sensitivity
features.description: Code should include an always @* block that references a wire named with a Verilog reserved keyword (initial) in its trigger condition, causing write_verilog to generate invalid code when the reserved keyword wire is also declared as a module port

features.name: initial as wire name in always @* trigger generation
features.description: Code should include an always @* block with an if condition using a wire named 'initial' (a reserved keyword) that is also declared as a module input port, where write_verilog incorrectly generates trigger code that conflicts with the module port declaration
features.snippet:
wire input 0 \initial;
always @* begin
  if (\initial) begin end
end
